title: Connector assembly
abstract: A penetrator connector assembly is suitable for connecting two parts of an electrical conductor across a pressure barrier in an oil or gas well. The assembly includes a body (30) with a bore (30b) for an electrical conductor (5c), a sealing boot (40) that seals an annular space between the conductor (5c) and the bore (30b), and a port (30p) that transmits a pressure differential between the exterior of the body and the annular space, so that the pressure differential acts on one side of the sealing boot (40) to apply a compressive force and enhance the seal. The assembly also has a spring (70) that can be resiliently energised to apply a compressive force to the sealing boot (40) independently of fluid pressure differentials.
claims:
Claims A connector assembly for connecting electrical conductors on opposite sides of a pressure barrier in an oil or gas well, the assembly comprising a body having a bore adapted to receive an electrical conductor within the bore, a sealing device adapted to seal an annular space between the electrical conductor and the bore, and a fluid pathway adapted to allow fluid communication between an outer surface of the body and the annular space, wherein the fluid pathway comprises at least one port, and wherein the port is adapted to transmit a pressure differential between the external surface of the body and the annular space to act on one side of the sealing device. A connector assembly as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the at least one port passes through the body into the bore, wherein the fluid pathway is adapted to transmit wellbore pressure on one side of the pressure barrier to a surface of the sealing device on the same side of the pressure barrier. A connector assembly as claimed in claim 2, wherein an inner end of the port has an opening into the bore which is disposed between a portion of the sealing device and a lower end of the body. A connector assembly as claimed in any one of claims 1-3, wherein the annular space is sealed by the sealing device at one end of the annular space, and wherein the bore of the body comprises a secondary seal axially spaced from the sealing device; wherein the fluid pressure within the body on the said one end of the sealing device is equalised. A connector assembly as claimed in any one of claims 1-4, wherein the pressure differential that is communicated via the bore applies a compressive force to the sealing device, wherein the compressive force enhances the seal across the annular space. 6. A connector assembly as claimed in any one of claims 1-5, wherein the bore of the body is adapted to receive the sealing device, and wherein a resilient 

 compression device is adapted to compress the sealing device against an outer surface of the conductor and against an inner surface of the bore. 7. A connector assembly as claimed in claim 6, wherein the resilient compression device surrounds at least a portion of the conductor. 8. A connector assembly as claimed in claim 6 or claim 7, wherein the resilient compression device comprises at least one bearing device arranged to reduce friction during rotational movement of one part of the assembly relative to another part of the assembly. 9. A connector assembly as claimed in any one of claims 6-8, wherein the sealing device is axially spaced from the resilient compression device within the bore. 10. A connector assembly as claimed in any one of claims 6-9, wherein the resilient compression device is adapted to apply a compressive force in a direction parallel to an axis of the bore. 1 1. A connector assembly as claimed in any one of claims 6-10, wherein the resilient compression device applies a force on the sealing device which is independent of fluid pressure within the annular space. 12. A connector assembly as claimed in any one of claims 6-1 1 , wherein at pressure differential values below a given pressure threshold, the force applied by the resilient compression device exceeds the force applied to the sealing device by the pressure differential, and wherein when the pressure differential value is above the given pressure threshold, the force applied to the sealing device by the pressure differential exceeds the force applied to the sealing device by the resilient compression device. 13. A connector assembly as claimed in claim 12, wherein the sealing device is adapted to resist passage of fluid through the bore when compressed by the resilient compression device or the pressure differential. 14. A connector assembly as claimed in any one of claims 1-13, wherein the sealing device comprises an extended portion configured to surround at least a portion of the electrical conductor, wherein the extended portion of the sealing device extends from the lower end of the body of the sealing device. 15. A connector assembly as claimed in claim 14, wherein the extended portion of the sealing device is in fluid communication with the annular space, whereby the pressure differential communicated to the annular area via the fluid pathway applies pressure to the extended portion of the sealing device and forces the extended portion of the sealing device radially against the outer surface of the electrical conductor, thereby creating or enhancing a seal between the extended portion of the sealing device and the electrical conductor. 16. A connector assembly as claimed in any one of claims 1-15, wherein the sealing device is resilient. 17. A connector assembly as claimed in any one of claims 1-16, adapted to urge the sealing device radially in a direction perpendicular to an axis of the bore. 18. A connector assembly as claimed in any one of claims 1-17, adapted to urge the sealing device axially within the bore, towards one end of the bore. 19. A connector assembly as claimed in claim 18, wherein at least one of the sealing device and a section of the bore receiving the sealing device is tapered. 20. A connector assembly as claimed in claim 19, wherein the bore tapers inwards towards the longitudinal central axis of the bore as the bore extends through the body, at least in a portion of the bore which receives the sealing device, and wherein axial movement of the sealing device under the force applied by the resilient compression device causes the sealing device to be compressed radially by the tapered bore and compressed axially as a result of the force applied by the compression device. 21. A connector assembly as claimed in any one of claims 1-20, wherein the body comprises first and second portions connectable by a threaded arrangement, 

 wherein said threaded arrangement is adjustable in order to vary the force energising the resilient compression device. 22. A connector assembly as claimed in claim 21 , wherein the sealing device is received between the first and second portions. 23. A connector assembly as claimed in claim 21 or claim 22, wherein the compression device is resiliently energised by engaging the threads between the first and second portions, wherein the first and second portions approach one another and compress the resilient compression device and the sealing device between them. 24. A connector assembly as claimed in any one of claims 1-23, wherein the electrical conductor is adapted to transmit electrical power or signals. 25. A connector assembly as claimed in claim 24, wherein the electrical conductor comprises an outer sheath adapted to resist ingress of fluid. 26. A connector assembly as claimed in claim 25, wherein the outer sheath surrounds at least a portion of an inner sheath, wherein the inner sheath provides electrical insulation for a conductor, and wherein the inner sheath extends axially along the conductor beyond the axial length of the outer sheath. 27. A connector assembly as claimed in any one of claims 1-26, wherein the assembly comprises an insulating sleeve, wherein the insulating sleeve is disposed within the bore of the body of the assembly, and wherein the insulating sleeve comprises at least one inner bore adapted to receive the end terminal of at least one conductor cable. 28. A connector assembly as claimed in claim 27, wherein the end terminal of the conductor cable comprises at least one of a crimped terminal, at least one bearing ring, a locking ring, and a conductor pin. 29. A connector assembly as claimed in claim 28, wherein the crimped terminal is crimped to the conductor and wherein the bearing ring surrounds at least a portion of the conductor and at least a portion of the crimped terminal. 30. A connector assembly as claimed in claim 29, wherein the outer surface of the bearing ring is tapered radially inwards towards an axis of the bearing ring. 31. A connector assembly as claimed in claim 30, wherein the bearing ring is configured to engage with a shoulder extending radially into the bore, and wherein engagement of the bearing ring with the shoulder transfers a load from the crimped terminal onto the shoulder. 32. A connector assembly as claimed in any one of claims 1-31 , wherein the assembly comprises a securing device adapted to fix the conductor to the body of the connector assembly. 33. A connector assembly as claimed in any one of claims 1-32, wherein the sealing device comprises a resilient boot having a bore to receive the conductor. 34. A connector assembly as claimed in claim 33, wherein the resilient boot comprises at least one axially extended sheath for each conductor in the bore, wherein each at least one sheath has a bore which receives at least a part of a respective conductor. 35. A connector assembly as claimed in claim 34, including a spring sleeve urged by the resilient compression device towards the sealing device, wherein the spring sleeve has a bore adapted to receive each conductor in the bore. 36. A connector assembly as claimed in claim 35 when dependent on claim 34, wherein the bore in the sleeve has a first portion and a second portion having a narrower diameter than the first portion, and wherein the sleeve is configured to move axially over the axially extending sheath of the boot, and wherein the axially extending sheath of the boot is adapted to be compressed radially between the inner surface of the narrow portion of the bore of the sleeve and the outer surface of the conductor received in the bore of the sleeve. 37. A penetrator assembly for connecting electrical conductors on opposite sides of a pressure barrier in an oil or gas well, the penetrator having a body, the body having a bore adapted to receive an electrical conductor within the bore, and having a terminal attached onto the electrical conductor, the assembly having at least one sleeve within the annular space between the outer surface of the conductor and the inner surface of the bore, the penetrator assembly including a sealing device adapted to seal an annular space between the electrical conductor and the bore, and a fluid pathway adapted to allow fluid communication between an outer surface of the body and the annular space, wherein the fluid pathway comprises at least one port, and wherein the port is adapted to transmit a pressure differential between the external surface of the body and the annular space to act on one side of the sealing device. 38. A method of connecting first and second limbs of a conduit on opposite sides of a pressure barrier in an oil or gas well, the method comprising passing the first limb of the conduit into a body of a connector assembly, said body having a bore adapted to receive the first limb of the conduit within the bore, wherein the method includes the steps of sealing an annular space between the first limb of the conduit and the bore with a sealing device; communicating fluid between an outer surface of the body and the annular space through a fluid pathway, wherein the fluid pathway comprises at least one port; and transmitting a pressure differential through the port between the external surface of the body and the annular space to act on one side of the sealing device. 39. A method as claimed in claim 38, wherein the conduit comprises an electrical conduit having an electrical conductor adapted to transmit electrical power or signals, the method including terminating the electrical conductor by crimping an electrical terminal onto the conductor. 40. A method as claimed in claim 39, wherein the electrical terminal is crimped onto the conductor at the well. 41. A method as claimed in any one of claims 38-40, the method including inserting the or each terminated conductor into a pre-formed aperture within the assembly 

 body, said aperture being adapted to mate with a connector assembly on the other side of the pressure barrier for transfer of electrical power or signals across the pressure barrier. 42. A method as claimed in any one of claims 38-41 , the method including securing the conduit to the connector assembly. 43. A method as claimed in any one of claims 38-42, the method including resiliently energising a resilient compression device, and applying a compressive force from the resilient compression device to the sealing device within the annular space. 44. A method as claimed in any one of claims 38-43, including accommodating expansion, contraction and movement of the components in the bore of the connector assembly by compression and expansion of the resilient compression device.
